title: The Dog And The Water Lily. No Fable.
content:
آ آ آ آ The noon was shady, and soft airs
آ آ آ آ Swept Ouseï؟½s silent tide,
آ آ آ آ When, ï؟½scaped from literary cares,
آ آ آ آ I wanderï؟½d on his side.


آ آ آ آ My spaniel, prettiest of his race,
آ آ آ آ And high in pedigree
آ آ آ آ (Two nymphs[1] adornï؟½d with every grace
آ آ آ آ That spaniel found for me),


آ آ آ آ Now wantonï؟½d lost in flags and reeds,
آ آ آ آ Now starting into sight,
آ آ آ آ Pursued the swallow oï؟½er the meads
آ آ آ آ With scarce a slower flight.


آ آ آ آ It was the time when Ouse displayï؟½d
آ آ آ آ His lilies newly blown;
آ آ آ آ Their beauties I intent surveyï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And one I wishï؟½d my own.


آ آ آ آ With cane extended far I sought
آ آ آ آ To steer it close to land;
آ آ آ آ But still the prize, though nearly caught,
آ آ آ آ Escaped my eager hand.


آ آ آ آ Beau markï؟½d my unsuccessful pains
آ آ آ آ With fixï؟½d considerate face,
آ آ آ آ And puzzling set his puppy brains
آ آ آ آ To comprehend the case.


آ آ آ آ But with a cherup clear and strong
آ آ آ آ Dispersing all his dream,
آ آ آ آ I thence withdrew, and followï؟½d long
آ آ آ آ The windings of the stream.


آ آ آ آ My ramble ended, I returnï؟½d;
آ آ آ آ Beau, trotting far before,
آ آ آ آ The floating wreath again discernï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And plunging, left the shore.


آ آ آ آ I saw him with that lily croppï؟½d
آ آ آ آ Impatient swim to meet
آ آ آ آ My quick approach, and soon he droppï؟½d
آ آ آ آ The treasure at my feet.


آ آ آ آ Charmï؟½d with the sight, the world, I cried,
آ آ آ آ Shall hear of this thy deed:
آ آ آ آ My dog shall mortify the pride
آ آ آ آ Of manï؟½s superior breed:


آ آ آ آ But chief myself I will enjoin,
آ آ آ آ Awake at dutyï؟½s call,
آ آ آ آ To show a love as prompt as thine
آ آ آ آ To Him who gives me all.
meaning:
"The Dog And The Water Lily. No Fable." is a quintessential example of William Cowper's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
